question.text: An importer hedges 50% of USD 1,600,000 exposure at futures rate 81.50. Spot at payment is 82, futures at 82.05. What is the effective exchange rate?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Futures P&L on hedged half: 82.05 - 81.50 = +0.55. Hedged rate = 82 - 0.55 = 81.45. Unhedged rate = 82. Effective = (82 × 0.5) + (81.45 × 0.5) = 81.725. **The Trap:** Only 50% was hedged, so the effective rate is the weighted average of hedged and unhedged rates. **Cross-Exam Context:** Partial hedging is directly from the workbook example. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Full hedge would have given 81.45. No hedge would have given 82. Partial hedge gives a blended rate.
